page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs using the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, specifically the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gn.16xlarge demonstrates exceptional multi-core and memory performance. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as strong, while Geekbench multi-core scores and software compilation with Clang reach top-tier levels. Memory bandwidth is a significant strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 7390.11 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 235.58 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU physics tests also achieve top-tier marks. Conversely, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong categories, respectively.

The server exhibits highly efficient performance for database and web-serving workloads. PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations, which reached 140.57 million operations per second, both rank in the top tier. Static web serving throughput is also top-tier at 115.57 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the instance shows mixed results; while text generation for a medium 7B LLM model is top-tier at 41.62 tokens/sec, text generation for a small 135M model is poor, and prompt processing across various model sizes remains average. Given its high network bandwidth, balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, and strong multi-core efficiency, the m8gn.16xlarge is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores (64 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, 256.0 GB of system memory, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression. It is highly efficient for database and caching workloads, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks, alongside top-tier static web serving throughput. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are more moderate, its high network capacity and balanced resource profile make it an excellent fit for high-throughput web serving, distributed caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.16xlarge is a 64-core ARM64 general-purpose instance with 256 GB RAM, 200 Gbps network speed, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering 200 Gbps network bandwidth and top-tier multi-core memory performance for high-throughput database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
High-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core software compilation performance
Excellent database and caching efficiency with top-tier Redis and web serving throughput
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded workloads
